Title: Ensure Administrative accounts are separate and cloud-only
Remediation: 1. Navigate to Microsoft 365 admin center 2. Click to expand Users select Active users.3. Sort by the Licenses column.4. For each user account in an administrative role verify the following: The account is Cloud only (not synced) The account is assigned a license that is not associated with applications i.e. (Microsoft Entra ID P1, Microsoft Entra ID P2)

Title: Ensure the admin consent workflow is enabled
Remediation: In the Microsoft 365 Admin Center, Select Admin Centers, and Microsoft Entra ID. Select Enterprise applications from the Azure Navigation pane. Under Security select Consent and permissions. Under Manage select Admin consent settings and set Users can request admin consent to apps they are unable to consent to Yes. Under the Reviewers choose the Roles, Groups that you would like to review user generated app consent requests. Select Save at the top of the window.

Title: Ensure custom banned passwords lists are used
Remediation: Create a custom banned password list:  Navigate to Microsoft Entra ID admin center https://entra.microsoft.com/ Click to expand Microsoft Entra ID > Protect & Secure > Authentication methods Select Password protection Set Enforce custom list to Yes In Custom banned password list create a list using suggestions outlined in this document. Click Save  NOTE: Below is a list of examples that can be used as a starting place. Check the references section for more.  Brand names Product names Locations, such as company headquarters Company-specific internal terms Abbreviations that have specific company meaning

Title: Ensure 'Microsoft Azure Management' is limited to administrative roles
Remediation: To enable Microsoft Azure Management restrictions:  Navigate to the Microsoft Entra ID admin center https://entra.microsoft.com. Click expand Protection > Conditional Access select Policies. Click New Policy and then name the policy. Select Users > Include > All Users Select Users > Exclude > Directory roles and select only administrative roles (See below). Select Cloud apps or actions > Select apps > Select then click the box next to Windows Azure Service Management API. Click Select. Select Grant > Block access and click Select. Ensure Enable Policy is On then click Create.  WARNING: Exclude Global Administrator at a minimum to avoid being locked out. Report-only is a good option to use when testing any Conditional Access policy for the first time. Below is the list of administrative roles that can be excluded:  Application administrator Authentication administrator Billing administrator Cloud application administrator Conditional Access administrator Exchange administrator Global administrator Global reader Helpdesk administrator Password administrator Privileged authentication administrator Privileged role administrator Security administrator SharePoint administrator User administrator  Default Value: No - Non-administrators can access the Microsoft Entra ID administration portal.

Title: Ensure 'LinkedIn account connections' is disabled
Remediation: To disable LinkedIn account connections:  Navigate to Microsoft Entra ID admin center https://entra.microsoft.com/ Click to expand Microsoft Entra ID > Users select User settings. Under LinkedIn account connections select No. Click Save at the top of the page.

Title: Ensure that only organizationally managed/approved public groups exist
Remediation: In the Microsoft 365 Administration portal, go to:Teams & groups -> Active teams & groups. For each group, under Settings, make sure Privacy is set to 'Private'.

Title: Ensure password protection is enabled for on-prem Active Directory
Remediation: To setup Microsoft Entra ID Password Protection, use the following steps:   Download and install the Microsoft Entra ID Password Proxies and DC Agents from the following location: https://www.microsoft.com/download/details.aspx?id=57071 After the installation is complete, login to https://admin.microsoft.com as a Global Administrator. Go to Admin centers and click on Microsoft Entra ID. Select Microsoft Entra ID then Security on the left side navigation followed by Authentication methods. Select Password protection and toggle Enable password protection on Windows Server Active Directory to Yes and Mode to Enforced Click Save at the top of the right pane.

Title: Ensure 'Phishing-resistant MFA strength' is required for Administrators
Remediation: To create a phishing-resistant MFA CA policy for users in administrative roles:  Navigate to the Microsoft Entra ID admin center https://entra.microsoft.com. Click to expand Microsoft Entra ID > Applications select Enterprise Applications. Under Security, select Conditional Access. Click New policy. Go to Users > Users and groups > Include > Select users and groups > Directory roles Add at least the Directory roles listed after these steps. Select Cloud apps or actions > All cloud apps (and don't exclude any apps). Grant > Grant Access with Require authentication strength (Preview): Phishing-resistant MFA Click 'Select' Set Enable policy to Report-only and click Create  At minimum these directory roles should be included for the policy:  Application administrator Authentication administrator Billing administrator Cloud application administrator Conditional Access administrator Exchange administrator Global administrator Global reader Helpdesk administrator Password administrator Privileged authentication administrator Privileged role administrator Security administrator SharePoint administrator User administrator  WARNING: Ensure administrators are pre-registered with strong authentication before enforcing the policy. After which the policy must be set to “On”.

Title: Ensure Sign-in frequency is enabled and browser sessions are not persistent for Administrative users
Remediation: To configure Sign-in frequency and browser sessions persistence for Administrative users:  Navigate to Microsoft Entra admin center https://entra.microsoft.com/.Click to Protection > Conditional Access. Click New policy Click Users and groups Under Include select Select users and groups and then select Directory roles. At a minimum, select the roles in the section below: Go to Target resources > Cloud apps > Include > select All cloud apps (and don't exclude any apps). Under Access controls > Grant > select Grant access > check Require multi-factor authentication (and nothing else). Under Session select Sign-in frequency and set to at most 4 hours for E3 tenants. E5 tenants with PIM can be set to a maximum value of 24 hours. Check Persistent browser session then select Never persistent in the drop-down menu. For Enable Policy select On and click Save  At minimum these directory roles should be included for MFA:  Application administrator Authentication administrator Billing administrator Cloud application administrator Conditional Access administrator Exchange administrator Global administrator Global reader Helpdesk administrator Password administrator Privileged authentication administrator Privileged role administrator Security administrator SharePoint administrator User administrator

Title: Ensure third party integrated applications are not allowed
Remediation: In the Microsoft 365 Admin Center Select Admin Centers and Microsoft Entra ID.Select Users from the Azure navigation paneSelect Users settings.Set App registrations is set to No.Click Save.

Title: Locate accounts in built-in Operator Groups
Remediation: 1. Review the exposed accounts and groups with membership in built-in operator groups.  2. Remove them from the Operator group directly in Active Directory.  3. If the membership is through a nested group, either remove the account from the nested group or remove the nested group from the operator group.

Title: Accounts with non-default Primary Group ID
Remediation: 1. Review the list of exposed entities to discover which of your accounts have a suspicious primaryGroupId.   2. Take appropriate action on those accounts by resetting their attribute to their default values or adding the member to the relevant group:  - user accounts: 513 (Domain Users) or 514 (Domain Guests)  - computer accounts: 515 (Domain Computers)  - domain controller accounts: 516 (Domain Controllers)  - read-only domain controller (RODC) accounts: 521 (Read-only Domain Controllers).   To achieve the full score, remediate all exposed entities.

Title: Change password for accounts with leaked credentials
Remediation: It is recommended to Enable Microsoft Entra ID Identity Protection user risk policies to automatically require users to change passwords when their credentials are leaked.  		Alternatively, you can follow the steps below to change password for individual users. 		1. Sign in to the Admin Center: 		Sign in to the Microsoft Entra Admin Center (entra.microsoft.com) with an administrator account. 		2. Navigate to Users: 		Select "Users" from the left-hand navigation menu. 		3. Select the User Account: 		Find and select the user account that needs a password change. 		4. Reset Password: 		Click on "Reset password" and choose whether to auto-generate a password or manually set a new password.

Title: Change password for on-prem account with potentially leaked credentials
Remediation: 1. Sign in to the Admin Console:     Open the Active Directory Users and Computers (ADUC) console using an administrator account.     2. Navigate to Users:     In ADUC: Navigate to the organizational unit (OU) where the user account is located.     3. Select the User Account:     Find and select the user account that needs a password change.     4. Reset Password:     Right-click on the user account, select "Reset Password," enter the new password, and confirm it.

Title: Prevent Certificate Enrollment with arbitrary Application Policies (ESC15)
Remediation: 1. Identify the vulnerable certificate templates:-Remove enrollment permission for unprivileged users.-Disable the “Supply in the request” option.2. Identify the AD CS servers which are vulnerable to CVE-2024-49019 and apply the relevant patch.

Title: Prevent users to request a certificate valid for arbitrary users based on the certificate template (ESC1)
Remediation: Identify the vulnerable certificate template. Perform at least one of the following possible remediations: 1. Disable the “Supply in the request” configuration. 2. Remove EKU’s enabling user authentication (e.g Client Authentication). 3.Remove overly permissive enrollment permissions, which allows any user to enroll certificate based on that certificate template. 4.Enable “CA certificate manager approval” requirement.

Title: Install Defender for Identity Sensor on ADCS servers
Remediation: Go to the sensor page  in Settings, you can view the already installed sensors in your environment and download the install package to deploy them on your remaining servers.For more details, see Configure sensors for AD FS, AD CS, and Microsoft Entra Connect .You will be scored as a percentage of your deployment progress.

Title: Edit insecure certificate enrollment IIS endpoints (ESC8)
Remediation: For each endpoint, follow these steps: 1. Determine whether the endpoint is necessary and in regular use. If it is not used, it is advisable to disable it. 2. Deactivate NTLM and Negotiate authentication providers for the IIS endpoint. 3. If NTLM cannot be disabled, enable "Require SSL" and "Require Extended Protection" for the IIS endpoint.For more information, please refer to the security advisory in the "Learn More" section.

Title: Edit misconfigured Certificate Authority ACL (ESC7)
Remediation: Identify the misconfigured ACL entry (for example - “Manage CA” permission granted to the “authenticated users” built-in group) and remove any unnecessary permissions granting built-in groups “Mange CA” or/and “Manage certificates” permissions.

Title: Edit misconfigured certificate templates ACL (ESC4)
Remediation: Identify the vulnerable certificate template, and remove overly permissive modifications permissions (e.g. WriteProperty), which allows any user to change the settings of that certificate template.

Title: Edit overly permissive Certificate Template with privileged EKU (Any purpose EKU or No EKU) (ESC2)
Remediation: Identify the vulnerable certificate template. Perform at least one of the following possible remediations: 1.Remove EKU’s enabling arbitrary usage (e.g. Any purpose). 2.Remove overly permissive enrollment permissions, which allows any user to enroll certificate based on that certificate template. 3.Enable “CA certificate manager approval” requirement.

Title: Edit misconfigured enrollment agent certificate template (ESC3)
Remediation: Identify the vulnerable certificate template. Perform at least one of the following possible remediations: 1.Remove enrollment agent EKU. 2.Remove overly permissive enrollment permissions, which allows any user to enroll certificate based on that certificate template. 3.Enable “CA certificate manager approval” requirement.

Title: Edit misconfigured certificate templates owner (ESC4)
Remediation: Edit misconfigured certificate templates owner (ESC4)

Title: Enforce encryption for RPC certificate enrollment interface (ESC11)
Remediation: To require packet encryption for RPC enrollment, execute the following command and then restart the certsvc service: certutil -setreg CA\InterfaceFlags +IF_ENFORCEENCRYPTICERTREQUEST net stop certsvc & net start certsvc

Title: Edit vulnerable Certificate Authority setting (ESC6)
Remediation: To disable “user specifying SAN” setting, execute the following command and then restart the certsvc service: certutil -setreg policy\EditFlags -EDITF_ATTRIBUTESUBJECTALTNAME2 net stop certsvc & net start certsvc

Title: Install Defender for Identity Sensor on ADFS servers
Remediation: Go to the sensor page  in Settings, you can view the already installed sensors in your environment and download the install package to deploy them on your remaining servers.For more details, see Configure sensors for AD FS, AD CS, and Microsoft Entra Connect .You will be scored as a percentage of your deployment progress.

Title: Remove access rights on suspicious accounts with the Admin SDHolder permission
Remediation: 1. Review the list of exposed entities to discover which of your non-sensitive accounts have the Admin SDHolder permission. 2. Take appropriate action on those entities by removing their privileged access rights. To achieve the full score, remediate all exposed entities.

Title: Change password for Entra seamless SSO account
Remediation: For each account, follow these steps:  1.Review this list of exposed entities to discover which of your accounts have an old password. 2.Take appropriate action on those entities by following the steps described here: https://aka.ms/RollOverAzureadssoAccount.

Title: Change password of built-in domain Administrator account
Remediation: 1. Review the list of exposed entities to discover which of your built-in domain Administrator accounts have an old password. 2. Take appropriate action on those accounts by resetting their password.To achieve the full score, remediate all exposed entities.

Title: Built-in Active Directory Guest account is enabled
Remediation: 1. Review the list of exposed entities to discover if there is a Guest account which is enabled.  2. Take appropriate action on those accounts by disabling the account.

Title: Change password for krbtgt account
Remediation: 1. Review the list of exposed entities to discover which of your krbtgt accounts have an old password.2. Take appropriate action on those accounts by resetting their password twice to invalidate the Golden Ticket attack. To achieve the full score, remediate all exposed entities.

Title: Stop clear text credentials exposure
Remediation: 1. Review this security control list of exposed entities.  2. Research why those entities are using LDAP in clear text.  3. Remediate the issues and stop the exposure.  4. After confirming remediation, we recommend you configure the requirement of domain controller level LDAP signing.  To achieve a full score, you must remediate all exposed entities.

Title: Remove stale CyberArk Identity privileged accounts
Remediation: 1.Review the list of exposed entities to discover which of your CyberArk Identity privileged user accounts have not been used for an extended period.     2.If the account is no longer required, deactivate or remove it to eliminate unnecessary exposure.

Title: High number of CyberArk Identity accounts with a privileged role assigned
Remediation: 1. Reduce the number of users assigned to other Admin roles to meet the recommended best practices and ensure better control.

Title: Limit the number of CyberArk Identity accounts with system admin role
Remediation: 1. Reduce the number of System Admin accounts to meet recommended best practices and ensure better control.

Title: Change password for CyberArk Identity  privileged User accounts
Remediation: 1. Review the list of exposed entities to discover which of your CyberArk Identity privileged user accounts have an old password.      2. Take appropriate action on those accounts by resetting their password.

Title: Start your Defender for Identity deployment, installing Sensors on Domain Controllers and other eligible servers.
Remediation: Go to the activation page in Settings, where you can view the discovered servers recommended for onboarding to the Defender for Identity new sensor. To see the sensors already installed in your environment, go to the sensor page.  You will be scored as a percentage of your deployment progress.

Title: Unsafe permissions on the DnsAdmins group
Remediation: 1. Review the list of exposed entities to identify non-privileged accounts with risky permissions. 2. Take appropriate action on those accounts by removing the accounts from the DnsAdmins group. If some accounts require these permissions, grant them only the specific access needed. To achieve the full score, remediate all exposed entities.

Title: Remove local admins on identity assets
Remediation: 1. Review this list of exposed entities to discover which of your accounts have local admin rights on your identity assets. 2. Take appropriate action on those entities by removing their privileged access rights. To achieve the full score, remediate all exposed entities.

Title: Change Domain Controller computer account old password
Remediation: 1. Verify Registry Values:  HKLM\System\CurrentControlSet\Services\Netlogon\Parameters\DisablePasswordChange is set to 0 or is nonexistent.  HKLM\System\CurrentControlSet\Services\Netlogon\Parameters\MaximumPasswordAge is set to 30.  2. Reset Incorrect Values: Reset any incorrect values to their default settings.   Check Group Policy Objects (GPOs) to ensure they do not override these settings.  3. If these values are correct, check if the NETLOGON service is started with sc.exe query netlogon.  4. Validate Password Synchronization by Running nltest /SC_VERIFY: (with DomainName being the domain NetBIOS name) can check the synchronization status and should display0 0x0 NERR_Success for both verifications.

Title: Remove dormant accounts from sensitive groups
Remediation: 1. Review this security control list of exposed entities to discover which of your sensitive accounts are dormant.. 2. Take appropriate action on those entities by removing their privileged access rights or by deleting the account.  To achieve a full score, you must remediate all exposed entities.

Title: Rotate password for Entra Connect AD DS Connector account
Remediation: 1. Review the list of exposed entities to discover which of your AD DS Connector accounts have a password more than 90 days old. 2. Take appropriate action on those accounts by following the steps described here: https://aka.ms/EntraIdPasswordChangeSyncService

Title: Replace Enterprise or Domain Admin account for Entra Connect AD DS Connector account
Remediation: 1. Review the exposed account and their group memberships. The list contains members of Domain/Enterprise Admins through direct and recursive membership. 2. Perform one of the following actions:   a. Remove the user from privileged groups, ensuring it retains the necessary permissions to function as the Entra Connect Connector account.  b. Change the Entra Connect AD DS Connector account.

Title: Remove unnecessary replication permissions for Entra Connect AD DS Connector Account
Remediation: 1. Review the list of exposed entities to discover which of your AD DS Connector accounts have replication permissions. 2. Take appropriate action on those accounts by removing their 'Replication Directory Changes' and 'Replication Directory Changes All' permissions.  For environments with multiple Entra Connect servers, it’s crucial to install sensors on each server to ensure Microsoft Defender for Identity can fully monitor your setup. It has been detected that your Entra Connect configuration does not utilize Password Hash Sync (PHS), which means that replication permissions are not necessary for the accounts in the Exposed Entities list.

Title: Install Defender for Identity Sensor on Entra Connect servers
Remediation: Go to the sensor page  in Settings, you can view the already installed sensors in your environment and download the install package to deploy them on your remaining servers.For more details, see Configure sensors for AD FS, AD CS, and Microsoft Entra Connect .You will be scored as a percentage of your deployment progress.

Title: Remove unsafe permissions on sensitive Entra Connect accounts
Remediation: 1. Review the list of exposed entities to identify accounts with unsafe permissions. 2. Remove problematic permissions that allows unprivileged accounts to takeover critical hybrid assets. To achieve the full score, remediate all exposed entities.

Title: Remove discoverable passwords in Active Directory account attributes
Remediation: 1. Review the exposed entries in the security report. Identify any field content that includes:    a. Cleartext passwords.    b. Reset instructions or credential clues    c. Sensitive business or system information2. Remove any sensitive information from the listed attribute fields using standard directory management tools (e.g., PowerShell, ADSI Edit).3. Change the account password.

Title: Set a valid password rotation interval for gMSA
Remediation: To update the password change interval for a Group Managed Service Account (gMSA), you'll need to create a new gMSA with the desired interval, as the interval can only be set during creation.Here's a quick overview of the steps:  1. Create a KDS Root Key(if not already created)  2. Create the new gMSA with the desired password change interval of 45 days  3. Install the new gMSA on the target machine  4. Verify the new gMSA

Title: GPO can be modified by unprivileged accounts
Remediation: Carefully review each assigned permission, identify any dangerous permission granted, and modify them to remove any unnecessary or excessive user rights. For more information, please refer to the security advisory in the "Learn More" section.

Title: GPO assigns unprivileged identities to local groups with elevated privileges
Remediation: Carefully review each assigned group membership, identify any dangerous group membership granted, and modify the GPO to remove any unnecessary or excessive user rights.

Title: Reversible passwords found in GPOs
Remediation: In order to remove the preferences that contain passwords data, use Group Policy Management Console (GPMC) on a domain controller or from a client that has Remote Server Administration Tools (RSAT) installed. You can remove any preference following these steps: 1.In GPMC, open the Group Policy reported in the Exposed entities tab. 2.Navigate to the preference configuration that contains password data and delete the object. Click Apply and OK to save your changes. 3.Wait a Group Policy refresh cycle to allow changes to propagate to clients (usually up to 120 minutes) 4.After changes are applied to all clients, delete the preference. 5.Repeat steps 1 through 5 as needed to clean your whole environment. For more information, please refer to the security advisory in the "Learn More" section.

Title: Set a honeytoken account
Remediation: Go to the  Honeytoken Settings page and set at least one honeytoken account.​

Title: Remove stale service accounts
Remediation: 1. Disable the account: Prevent any usage by disabling the account.      2. Monitor for impact: Wait several weeks and monitor for any operational issues (e.g., service failures, errors).      3. Delete the account: If no issues are reported, proceed with deletion.

Title: Modify unsecure Kerberos delegations to prevent impersonation
Remediation: 1. Review this security control list of exposed entities to discover which of your non-domain controller entities are configured for unsecure Kerberos delegation.  2. Take appropriate action on those at-risk users, such as removing their unconstrained attribute or changing it to a more secure constrained delegation.  To achieve a full score, you must remediate all exposed entities.

Title: Remove non-admin accounts with DCSync permissions
Remediation: 1. Review this list of exposed entities to discover which of your accounts have DCSync permissions and are also non-domain admins. 2. Take appropriate action on those entities by removing their privileged access rights. To achieve the full score, remediate all exposed entities.

Title: Remove dormant Okta privileged accounts
Remediation: 1. Review the list of exposed entities to discover which of your Okta privileged user accounts have not been used for an extended period. 2. If the account is no longer required, deactivate or remove it to eliminate unnecessary exposure. To achieve a full score, you must remediate all exposed entities.

Title: High number of Okta accounts with privileged role assigned
Remediation: Reduce the number of users assigned to other Administrator role to meet recommended best practices and ensure better control.

Title: Limit the Number of Okta Super Admins Accounts
Remediation: 1. Reduce the number of super admin accounts.

Title: Change password for Okta privileged User accounts
Remediation: 1. Review the list of exposed entities to discover which of your Okta privileged user accounts have an old password. 2. Take appropriate action on those accounts by resetting their password.

Title: Assign multi-factor authentication for Okta privileged user accounts
Remediation: 1. Review the list of exposed entities to discover which of your Okta privileged user accounts do not have any MFA method assigned. 2. Assign and enforce a multi-factor authentication (MFA) method to the privileged accounts.

Title: Highly Privileged Okta Api Token
Remediation: 1. Review the list of privileged Okta API tokens. 2. Remove unused API tokens.

Title: Reduce lateral movement path risk to sensitive entities
Remediation: Review this security control list of exposed entities displaying sensitive entities with lateral movement paths risk with recommended actions on how to reduce the number of non-sensitive accounts for each path.  To achieve a full score, you must remediate all exposed entities.

Title: Remove stale PingOne privileged accounts
Remediation: 1. Review the list of exposed entities to discover which of your PingOne privileged user accounts have not been used for an extended period. 2. If the account is no longer required, deactivate or remove it to eliminate unnecessary exposure. To achieve a full score, you must remediate all exposed entities.

Title: High number of PingOne accounts with a privileged role assigned
Remediation: 1. Reduce the number of users assigned to other Admin roles to meet the recommended best practices and ensure better control.

Title: Limit the number of PingOne accounts with organization admin role
Remediation: 1. Reduce the number of users assigned to Organization Admin role to meet the recommended best practices and ensure better control.

Title: Change password for PingOne privileged User accounts
Remediation: 1. Review the list of exposed entities to discover which of your PingOne privileged user accounts has an old password. 2. Take appropriate action on those accounts by resetting their password.

Title: Assign multi-factor authentication for PingOne privileged user accounts
Remediation: 1. Review the list of exposed entities to discover which of your PingOne privileged user accounts do not have any MFA method assigned. 2. Assign and enforce a multi-factor authentication (MFA) method for all the privileged accounts.

Title: Disable Print spooler service on domain controllers
Remediation: 1. Review this security control list of exposed entities to discover which of your domain controllers has the Print spooler service enabled. 2. Take appropriate action on the at-risk domain controllers and actively remove the Print spooler service either manually, through group policy or other types of remote commands.  Make sure to investigate your Print spooler settings, configurations and dependencies before disabling this service and preventing active printing workflows.  To achieve a full score, you must remediate all exposed entities.

Title: Ensure privileged accounts are not delegated
Remediation: 1. Review the list of exposed entities to discover which of your privileged user accounts don’t have the configuration flag "this account is sensitive and cannot be delegated".  2. Take appropriate action on those accounts by setting the account's control flags to "this account is sensitive and cannot be delegated". This will prevent users from gaining access to the account and manipulating system settings.  3. For device accounts, enable "not delegated" setting.    To achieve the full score, remediate all exposed entities.

Title: Identify Entra ID privileged accounts that are also privileged in Active Directory
Remediation: 1. Review each dual-privileged account and determine whether access is required in both environments. 2. Remove unnecessary privileges from either Entra ID or Active Directory to reduce exposure.3. Consider separating cloud and on-prem roles across different accounts or implementing just-in-time access. 4. Use Entra Privileged Identity Management (PIM) to enforce approval of workflows and limit standing access.

Title: Protect and manage local admin passwords with Microsoft LAPS
Remediation: 1. Review this security control list of domains and devices not configured for Microsoft LAPS.  2. Deploy Microsoft LAPS to actively manage the local administrator password of your devices. This is done through Active Directory along with Group Policy client- side extensions that will be installed on each Windows operating system.  To achieve a full score, you must remediate all exposed entities.

Title: Remove stale SailPoint privileged accounts
Remediation: 1. Review the list of exposed entities to discover which of your SailPoint privileged user accounts have not been used for an extended period.      2. If the account is no longer required, deactivate or remove it to eliminate unnecessary exposure.

Title: High number of SailPoint accounts with a privileged role assigned
Remediation: 1. Reduce the number of users assigned to other Admin roles to meet the recommended best practices and ensure better control.

Title: Limit the number of SailPoint accounts with system admin role
Remediation: 1. Reduce the number of users assigned to the System Admin role to meet the recommended best practices and ensure better control.

Title: Assign multi-factor authentication for SailPoint privileged user accounts
Remediation: 1. Review the list of exposed entities to discover which of your SailPoint privileged user accounts do not have any MFA method assigned.       2. Assign and enforce a multi-factor authentication (MFA) method for all the privileged accounts.

Title: Change password for SailPoint privileged User accounts
Remediation: 1. Review the list of exposed entities to discover which of your SailPoint privileged user accounts has an old password.      2. Take appropriate action on those accounts by resetting their password.

Title: Install Defender for Identity Sensor on all Domain Controllers
Remediation: Go to the activation page in Settings, where you can view the discovered servers recommended for onboarding to the Defender for Identity new sensor. To see the sensors already installed in your environment, go to the sensor page.  You will be scored as a percentage of your deployment progress.

Title: Identify service accounts in privileged groups
Remediation: 1. Review each service account in the exposed entities list and validate its need for privileged access. 2. Remediate the account by either removing it from the privileged group or disabling the account if it is confirmed to be unused.

Title: Remove unsecure SID history attributes from entities
Remediation: 1. Review this security control list of exposed entities that has unsecure SID history attributes. 2. Take appropriate action on those entities by removing their unsecure settings.  To achieve a full score, you must remediate all exposed entities.

Title: Rotate old password for sMSA and set up valid rotation interval in the GPO
Remediation: Update password for the sMSA:  1. Open PowerShell with administrative privileges   2. Use the Set-ADServiceAccount Cmdlet to update the password  3. Verify the password update  It is recommended to define automated password change policy for the sMSA (e.g. using GPO or PAM solution)

Title: Remove stale Active Directory accounts
Remediation: 1. Review the list of exposed entities to discover which of your AD user accounts have not been used for an extended period. 2. If the account is no longer required, disable or delete it to eliminate unnecessary exposure.To achieve a full score, you must remediate all exposed entities.

Title: Resolve unsecure account attributes
Remediation: 1. Review this security control list of exposed entities that has unsecure account attributes. 2. Take appropriate action on those entities by removing their unsecure settings.  To achieve a full score, you must remediate all exposed entities.

Title: Resolve unsecure domain configurations
Remediation: 1. Review this security control list of exposed domains that have unsecure configurations. 2. Take appropriate action on those domains by remediating their unsecure settings. To achieve a full score, you must remediate all exposed entities.

Title: Configure VPN integration
Remediation: Go to the Settings page >, and configure the VPN radius accounting to enable VPN based detections.

Title: Stop weak cipher usage
Remediation: 1. Review this security control list of exposed entities.  2. Research why the identified clients and servers are using weak ciphers.  3. Remediate the issues and disable use of RC4 and/or other weak ciphers (such as DES/3DES).  To achieve a full score, you must remediate all exposed entities.

Title: Ensure 'User owned apps and services' is restricted
Remediation: To prohibit users installing Office Store add-ins and starting 365 trials:  Navigate to Microsoft 365 admin center https://admin.microsoft.com. Click to expand Settings Select Org settings.3. Under Services select User owned apps and services.4. Uncheck Let users access the Office Store and Let users start trials on behalf of your organization.5. Click Save.  Note - Due to temporary limitations, only "Let users access the Office Store" will be taken into account in scoring this control. It is suggested to uncheck both settings for the sake of better posture.

Title: Ensure multifactor authentication is enabled for all users in administrative roles
Remediation: We provide step-by-step guidance to select and enable the right MFA method for your organization in the Microsoft 365 admin center. Go to the Microsoft 365 MFA wizard If you would like to perform the implementation yourself, first check what Microsoft Entra ID license you have under “Prerequisites” in Microsoft Secure Score or see your license type under "Basic information" in the Microsoft Entra ID Overview. If you’ve invested in Microsoft Entra ID Premium P1 or P2 licenses, you can create a Conditional Access policy from scratch or by using a template. Follow these steps to create a Conditional Access policy from scratch or by using a template If you would like to perform the implementation yourself and you’re using Microsoft Entra ID Free, turn on security defaults. Note: Security defaults and Conditional Access can’t be used side by side. Enable security defaults Keep track of your admin’s progress of registering authentication methods by going to Microsoft Entra ID > Security > Authentication methods > User registration details (requires Microsoft Entra ID Premium P1 or P2 licenses). Go to User registration details

Title: Regulate cloud app access to sensitive data
Remediation: In app governance, open the predefined policy "Access to sensitive data" and activate it.               You can edit the predefined policy to specify an action and add exceptions.

Title: Regulate apps with priority account consent
Remediation: In app governance, open the predefined "Unusual activity from an app with priority account consent" policy and activate it.               While you have the policy open, you can add an action to take on unused apps and add exceptions.

Title: Enable Conditional Access policies to block legacy authentication
LicenseRequired: Microsoft Entra ID Premium Plan 1 (oder M365 Business Premium / E3 / E5)
Remediation: We provide step-by-step guidance to select and enable the right method to block legacy authentication for your organization in the Microsoft 365 admin center (part of the MFA wizard). Go to the Microsoft 365 MFA wizard If you would like to perform the implementation yourself, first check what Microsoft Entra ID license you have under “Prerequisites” in Microsoft Secure Score or see your license type under "Basic information" in the Microsoft Entra ID Overview. If you’ve invested in Microsoft Entra ID Premium P1 or P2 licenses, you can create a Conditional Access policy from scratch or by using a template. Follow these steps to create a Conditional Access policy from scratch or by using a template If you would like to perform the implementation yourself and you’re using Microsoft Entra ID Free, turn on security defaults. Note: Security defaults and Conditional Access can’t be used side by side. Enable security defaults

Title: Ensure the customer lockbox feature is enabled
LicenseRequired: Exchange Online Plan 1/2 (oder M365 Business Standard / E3 / E5)
Remediation: Turn on customer lockbox by:  1. Using a work or school account that has either the global administrator or the Customer Lockbox access approver role assigned, go to https://admin.microsoft.com and sign in.  2. Choose Settings > Org Settings.  3. Select Security & Privacy > Customer Lockbox > Edit, and then move the toggle to On or Off to turn the feature on or off.

Title: Ensure DLP policies are enabled
Remediation: Ensure that at least one Microsoft 365 DLP policy exists in the Microsoft 365 Compliance portal

Title: Ensure 'External sharing' of calendars is not available
LicenseRequired: Exchange Online Plan 1/2 (oder M365 Business Standard / E3 / E5)
Remediation: In the Microsoft 365 Exchange admin center, go to Organization > Sharing. Under Individual Sharing, make sure all policies are unticked.

Title: Ensure mailbox auditing for all users is Enabled
LicenseRequired: Exchange Online Plan 1/2 (oder M365 Business Standard / E3 / E5)
Remediation: To enable mailbox auditing for all users:  Connect to Exchange Online using Connect-ExchangeOnline. Run the following PowerShell command:Set-OrganizationConfig -AuditDisabled $false For each unconfigured MailBox of type Resource Mailboxes, Public Folder Mailboxes or DiscoverySearch Mailbox run:Get-Mailbox -Filter "Name -eq 'MailBox name'" | Set-Mailbox -AuditEnabled $true

Title: Ensure MailTips are enabled for end users
LicenseRequired: Exchange Online Plan 1/2 (oder M365 Business Standard / E3 / E5)
Remediation: To enable MailTips, use the Exchange Online PowerShell Module:  Run Microsoft Exchange Online PowerShell Module Connect using "Connect-ExchangeOnline" Run the following PowerShell command:  Set-OrganizationConfig -MailTipsAllTipsEnabled $true -MailTipsExternalRecipientsTipsEnabled $true -MailTipsGroupMetricsEnabled $true -MailTipsLargeAudienceThreshold '25'

Title: Ensure modern authentication for Exchange Online is enabled
LicenseRequired: Exchange Online Plan 1/2 (oder M365 Business Standard / E3 / E5)
Remediation: To enable modern authentication, use the Exchange Online PowerShell Module:  Run the Microsoft Exchange Online PowerShell Module. Connect to Exchange Online using "Connect-ExchangeOnline." Run the following PowerShell command: Set-OrganizationConfig -OAuth2ClientProfileEnabled $True

Title: Ensure users installing Outlook add-ins is not allowed
LicenseRequired: Exchange Online Plan 1/2 (oder M365 Business Standard / E3 / E5)
Remediation: To prohibit users installing Outlook add-ins:  Navigate to Exchange admin center https://admin.exchange.microsoft.com. Click to expand Roles select User roles. Select Default Role Assignment Policy. In the properties pane on the right click on Manage permissions. Under Other roles uncheck My Custom Apps, My Marketplace Apps and My ReadWriteMailboxApps. Click Save changes.  Note - This security control will take into account only the default policy. It is suggested to set the above for all the policies.

Title: Ensure that SPF records are published for all Exchange Domains
LicenseRequired: Exchange Online Plan 1/2 (oder M365 Business Standard / E3 / E5)
Remediation: follow the instructions on Set up SPF to help prevent spoofing - Office 365 | Microsoft Learn

Title: Ensure additional storage providers are restricted in Outlook on the web
LicenseRequired: Exchange Online Plan 1/2 (oder M365 Business Standard / E3 / E5)
Remediation: Restrict additional storage providers are restricted using PowerShell:  Connect to Exchange Online using Connect-ExchangeOnline. Run the following PowerShell command:Set-OwaMailboxPolicy -Identity OwaMailboxPolicy-Default -AdditionalStorageProvidersAvailable $false Run the following Powershell command to verify that the value is now False:Get-OwaMailboxPolicy | Format-Table Name, AdditionalStorageProvidersAvailable

Title: Ensure Spam confidence level (SCL) is configured in mail transport rules with specific domains
LicenseRequired: Exchange Online Plan 1/2 (oder M365 Business Standard / E3 / E5)
Remediation: To modify SCL in mail transport rules so they do not allow any specific domains:  Navigate to Exchange admin center https://admin.exchange.microsoft.com. Click to expand Mail Flow and then select Rules. For each rule that allows specific domains, set the spam confident level (SCL) to 0 or greater.  In "Do the following" section, select "Modify the message properties" and "set the spam confidence level (SCL)" and set to at least 0 (specifying the action for this domain, read more in the references attached below, some options may entirely block mail from this domain).    References:  Spam confidence level | Microsoft Learn Best practices for configuring mail flow rules in Exchange Online | Microsoft Learn Mail flow rules (transport rules) in Exchange Online | Microsoft Learn

Title: Ensure internal phishing protection for Forms is enabled
Remediation: To enable internal phishing protection for Forms:  Navigate to Microsoft 365 admin center https://admin.microsoft.com. 2. Click to expand Settings then select Org settings.3. Under Services select Microsoft Forms.4. Click the checkbox labeled Add internal phishing protection under Phishing protection.5. Click Save.

Title: Ensure user consent to apps accessing company data on their behalf is not allowed
Remediation: Go to Microsoft Entra ID > Enterprise applications > Consent and permissions. Go to Consent and permissions Select “Allow user consent for apps from verified publishers, for selected permissions (Recommended)“ to follow Microsoft’s best practice. Learn more Configure the low-impact permissions that users are allowed to consent to. Click “Select permissions to classify as low impact”. Learn more Optionally, you can help your users to consent to apps that require admin consent by setting up the admin consent workflow. This step is recommended but not required to get full score. Learn how to configure the admin consent workflow

Title: Ensure Microsoft Defender for Cloud Apps is enabled and configured
Remediation: Enable Defender for Cloud Apps by the following instructions: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/defender-cloud-apps/get-started

Title: Deploy a log collector to discover shadow IT activity
Remediation: In the Defender for Cloud Apps portal, go to the Automatic log upload page.   In the Data sources tab, select Add data source to create a data source for your appliance. In the Log collector tab, select Add log collector to add a new one. Follow the instructions provided to deploy Docker and the log collector container.

Title: Enable multi-factor authentication (MFA)
Remediation: To require two-step verification from an authentication policy:  Go to admin.atlassian.com. Select your organization if you have more than one. Select Security > Authentication policies. Select Edit for the policy you want to modify. On the Settings page, select Mandatory for "Two-step verification".   All policies must be configured this way to comply.

Title: Enable Single Sing On (SSO)
Remediation: Copy details from your identity provider to your Atlassian organization   Go to admin.atlassian.com. Select your organization if you have more than one.   Select Security > Identity providers.   Select your identity provider Directory.   Select Set up SAML single sign-on.   Add SAML details.   Save SAML configuration.

Title: Enable session timeout for web users
Remediation: To select an idle session duration from an authentication policy:   Go to admin.atlassian.com. Select your organization if you have more than one.   Select Security > Authentication policies.   Select Edit for the policy you want to modify.   On the Settings page, select length of time for Idle session duration.  The value should be equal to or lower than 2 hours.

Title: Atlassian mobile app security - App access requirement
Remediation: To set this policy:  Go to Security and Mobile app policy in the Atlassian admin dashboard. Click on "Create mobile app policy" or "edit" (in case a policy already exists). Mark all the three options:   Block compromised devices. Require data encryption. Require biometric authentication or a device passcode.   Note that all the three options must be checked in order to comply. Note that the 'minimum OS version' is not mandatory for this control, but the recommendation is to set it to the latest.

Title: Atlassian mobile app security - App data protection
Remediation: To set this policy:  Go to Security and Mobile app policy in the Atlassian admin dashboard. Click on "Create mobile app policy" or "edit" (in case a policy already exists). Mark all the three options:   Disable sharing, saving or backing up content from the mobile app. Disable screenshots and screen recording of the mobile app. Disable cutting or copying content from the mobile app.   Note that all the three options must be checked in order to comply.

Title: Atlassian mobile app security - Users that are affected by policies
Remediation: To set this policy:  Go to Security and Mobile app policy in the Atlassian admin dashboard. Click on "Create mobile app policy" or "edit" (in case a policy already exists). Set the policy to apply to "All users with access to your organization's products"

Title: Enable Password expiration policies
Remediation: To set this policy:In Atlassian admin dashboard Go to Security and Authentication policies for every policy select edit and go to "Passwords expire every", check it and set the day to 30 days or lower.

Title: Enable multi-factor authentication (MFA)
Remediation: In Citrix | Share file dashboard-  Go to "Settings>Admin Settings". Go to "Security>Login & Security Policy". In "Two-step verification" set the option on.

Title: Enable Single Sign on (SSO)
Remediation: In Citrix | Share file dashboard-  Go to "Settings>Admin Settings". Go to "Security>Login & Security Policy". In "Single sign-on / SAML 2.0 Configuration" set the option on.

Title: Enable session timeout for web users
Remediation: In Citrix | Share file dashboard-  Go to "Settings>Admin Settings". Go to "Security>Login & Security Policy". In "Authentication" set "log inactive users out after" at least 1 hour.

Title: Enhance 'login maximum attempts' - Lockout timer
Remediation: In Citrix | Share file dashboard-  Go to "Settings>Admin Settings". Go to "Security>Login & Security Policy". In "Account lock-out configuration" and set at least three hundred in "failed log in attempts".

Title: Enhance 'login maximum attempts' - Number of attempts
Remediation: In Citrix | Share file dashboard-  Go to "Settings>Admin Settings". Go to "Security>Login & Security Policy". In "Account lock-out configuration" and set at least five in "failed log in attempts".

Title: Enable password minimum length
Remediation: In Citrix | Share file dashboard-  Go to "Settings>Admin Settings". Go to "Security>Password Requirements". In "Requirements" set at least 8 in password minimum characters.

Title: Enable password minimum numeric characters
Remediation: In Citrix | Share file dashboard-  Go to "Settings>Admin Settings". Go to "Security>Password Requirements". In "Requirements" set at least 1 in number characters.

Title: Enable password minimum special characters
Remediation: In Citrix | Share file dashboard-  Go to "Settings>Admin Settings". Go to "Security>Password Requirements". In "Requirements" set at least 1 in special characters.

Title: Enable password expiration policies
Remediation: In Citrix | Share file dashboard-  Go to "Settings>Admin Settings". Go to "Security>Password Requirements". In "Requirements" set at least 90 in "The password expires after".

Title: Enhance password requirements
Remediation: In DocuSign dashboard -  Go to Account > Security setting in the side panel. Set "Login Requirements" -  Minimum password length of 9 characters. Password must include at least:   At least one uppercase character. At least one lowercase character. At least one digit. At least one special character (!, @, #...).   Have a minimum password age of 8 days.

Title: Password expiry requirements
Remediation: In DocuSign dashboard -   Go to Account > Security setting in the side panel. In Password Security, click on "Show Password Rules" button. In the pop-up window, set "Days until password expires" to 180 days.

Title: Enable session timeout for web users
Remediation: In DocuSign dashboard -  Go to Account > Security setting in the side panel. Set "Web App Session Timeout (minutes)" up to 120 minutes. Set "Mobile App Session Timeout (minutes)" up to 120 minutes. " Suppress In Session Certificate Enforcement" option must be set to false.

Title: Enable web session timeout for web users
Remediation: How to require session policies for Dropbox users  Sign in to dropbox.com with your admin credentials. Click Admin Console. Click Settings. Under Additional Settings, click Web session control. Set the setting next to Fixed session length to 1 week or less. It is also suggested, but not mandatory for compliance, to set the setting next to Idle session length to 30 minutes or less. Click Save changes.

Title: Disable 'Allow members to view dependency insights'
Remediation: In top right corner of GitHub, click your profile photo, then click Your enterprise > Next to the organization, click Settings. Go to Access > Member privileges. Disable "Allow members to view dependency insights" option in the "Member organization permissions" section.

Title: Enabled 'email notification delivery for this enterprise is restricted to verified or approved domains'
Remediation: In top right corner of GitHub, click your profile photo, then click Your enterprise > Next to the organization, click Settings. Go to Security > Verified & approved domains. Add a new domain and verify it. Check "Restrict email notifications to only approved or verified domains." option.

Title: Enforce IP allow list configuration for org resources
Remediation: In top right corner of GitHub, click your profile photo, then click Your enterprise > Next to the organization, click Settings. Go to Security > Authentication security. Check "Enable IP allow list configuration for installed GitHub Apps" option.

Title: Disable 'Allow repository administrators to invite outside collaborators to repositories for this organization
Remediation: In top right corner of GitHub, click your profile photo, then click Your enterprise > Next to the organization, click Settings. Go to Access > Member privileges. Disable "Allow repository administrators to invite outside collaborators to repositories for this organization" option in the "Admin repository permissions" section.

Title: Disable private repository forking
Remediation: In top right corner of GitHub, click your profile photo, then click Your enterprise > Next to the organization, click Settings. Go to Access > Member privileges. Disable "Allow forking of private and internal repositories" option.

Title: Disable 'Members will be able to create public repositories, visible to anyone'
Remediation: In top right corner of GitHub, click your profile photo, then click Your enterprise > Next to the organization, click Settings. Go to Access > Member privileges. Disable "Public" option in the "Repository creation" section.

Title: Disable 'members with admin permissions for repositories can delete or transfer repositories'
Remediation: In top right corner of GitHub, click your profile photo, then click Your enterprise > Next to the organization, click Settings. Go to Access > Member privileges. Disable "Allow members to delete or transfer repositories for this organization" option in the "Admin repository permissions" section.

Title: Disable 'Allow members to change repository visibilities for this organization'
Remediation: In top right corner of GitHub, click your profile photo, then click Your enterprise > Next to the organization, click Settings. Go to Access > Member privileges. Disable "Allow members to change repository visibilities for this organization" option in the "Admin repository permissions" section.

Title: Enable single sign on (SSO)
Remediation: On GitHub Enterprise organization page-  Go to Settings > Organization security Select Enable SAML authentication  Fill the fields with the values found in the Admin Portal

Title: Enable multi-factor authentication (MFA)
Remediation: Ensure password expiry policy for Google -  Navigate to Google admin center - http://admin.google.com/ Click Security > Password Management. Chcek Allow users to turn on 2-Step Verification. Check On under Enforcement. Click Save.  Follow steps 3-4 for every organizational unit.

Title: Adopt SSO (Single sign on) in netDocuments
Remediation: How does an organization start using federated identity with NetDocuments?  In the upper-right corner, select your name > Admin > repository name. In the Navigation Pane, select Security Center > Advanced Authentication. The Advanced Authentication Configuration page has a section titled Federated Identity. Choose the type of federated identity server you will use and configure it. Note: In order to comply with this control, at least 80% of the users must be using SSO.

Title: Enhance password requirements
Remediation: In Okta's dashboard -   Go to Security > Authentication panel. Under the "Password" tab.  Select "Edit" the policy and check the additional complexity requirement options. Lower case, letter Upper case, letter Number (0-9), Symbol (e.g., !@#$%^&*), Does not contain part of username, Does not contain first name and Does not contain last name.   "Minimum length" should be equal or above 8 characters.

Title: Enable multi-factor authentication
Remediation: In Okta's dashboard -   Go to Security > Multifactor panel. Go to "Factor Enrollment" and select "Add Multifactor Policy". Note: In order to comply, you must configure default policies as "required" ("optional" is not sufficient).

Title: Password expiry requirements
Remediation: In Okta's dashboard -   Go to Security > Authentication panel. Under the "Password" tab.  Select "Edit" the policy and check the additional Password age options - Enforce password history for last [Minimum of 3] passwords. Minimum password age is [Minimum of one day]. Password expires after [Minimum of 180 days] days. Prompt user [Minimum of five days] days before password expires. Lock out user after [Maximum of 5 attempts] unsuccessful attempts. Account is automatically unlocked after [Minimum of 15] minutes

Title: Enable session timeout for web users
Remediation: In Okta's dashboard -  Go to Security > Authentication panel. Go to "Sign On" If there isn't a policy - select "Add New Okta Sing-on Policy". If a policy exists - select  "Add rule "   In the window, go to  "Session management" and set the properties as following -   Maximum Okta session lifetime to 30 days or below.   Expire session after user has been idle on Okta for 120 minutes or below.  Persist session cookies across browser sessions to Disable.

Title: Remote Site
Disable Administrators Can Log In As Any User
Remediation: 1. From Setup, enter Login Access Policies in the Quick Findbox, then select Login Access Policies.2. Deselect Administrators Can Log in as Any User.

Title: Disable Caching and Autocomplete on Login Page via Session settings
Remediation: 1. From Setup, in the Quick Find box, enter Session Settings, then select Session Settings.2. Uncheck Enable caching and autocomplete on login page.

Title: Enable clickjack protection for non-Setup for Salesforce pages

Title: Enable clickjack protection for customer VisualForce pages with standard headers
Remediation: 1. From Setup, in the Quick Find box, enter Session Settings, then select Session Settings.2. Select Enable clickjack protection for customer Visualforce pages with standard headers.

Title: Enable clickjack protection for customer VisualForce pages with headers disabled
Remediation: 1. From Setup, in the Quick Find box, enter Session Settings, then select Session Settings.2. Select Enable clickjack protection for customer Visualforce pages with headers disabled.

Title: Enable Content Sniffing protection
Remediation: 1. From Setup, in the Quick Find box, enter Session Settings, then select Session Settings.2. Select Enable Content Sniffing protectio.

Title: Enable Content Security Policy protection for email templates

Title: Enable CSRF protection on GET requests on non-setup pages

Title: Enable CSRF protection on POST requests on non-setup pages
Remediation: 1. From Setup, in the Quick Find box, enter Session Settings, then select Session Settings.2. Select Enable CSRF protection on POST requests on non-setup pages..

Title: Require identity verification during multi-factor authentication (MFA) registration
Remediation: From Setup, in the Quick Find box, enter Identity, and then click Identity Verification. Make sure the field: "Require identity verification during multi-factor authentication (MFA) registration" is enabled.

Title: Let users verify their identity by text (SMS)
Remediation: 1. From Setup, in the Quick Find box, enter Identity, and then click Identity Verification.  2. Select Let users verify their identity by text (SMS).

Title: Enforce login IP ranges on every request
Remediation: 1. From Setup, in the Quick Find box, enter Session Settings, then select Session Settings.2. Select Enforce login IP ranges on every request.

Title: Force logout on session timeout
Remediation: 1. From Setup, in the Quick Find box, enter Session Settings, then select Session Settings.2. Select Force logout on session timeout.

Title: Force (admin) relogin after Login-As-User
Remediation: 1. From Setup, in the Quick Find box, enter Session Settings, then select Session Settings.2. Select Force relogin after Login-As-Use..

Remediation: 1. From Setup, in the Quick Find box, enter Identity, and then click Identity Verification.2. Select Require identity verification for email address changes.

Remediation: 1. From Setup, in the Quick Find box, enter Session Settings, then select Session Settings.  2. Select Lock sessions to the domain in which they were first used.

Title: Maximum invalid login attempts
Remediation: 1. From Setup, enter Password Policies in the Quick Find box, then select Password Policies.2. Under Maximum invalid login attempts, select 3.

Title: Require a minimum 1 day password lifetime
Remediation: 1. From Setup, enter Password Policies in the Quick Find box, then select Password Policies.2. Select Require a minimum 1 day password lifetime.

Title: Password complexity requirement
Remediation: 1. From Setup, enter Password Policies in the Quick Find box, then select Password Policies.2. Under Password complexity requirement, select Must mix alpha, numeric, and special characters, or more complex.

Title: User passwords expire in 90 days or less
Remediation: 1. From Setup, enter Password Policies in the Quick Find box, then select Password Policies.2. Under User passwords expire in, select 90 days or less.

Title: Enforce password history
Remediation: 1. From Setup, enter Password Policies in the Quick Find box, then select Password Policies.2. Under Enforce password history, select 3 or more passwords rememebered.

Title: Lockout effective period
Remediation: 1. From Setup, enter Password Policies in the Quick Find box, then select Password Policies.2. Under Lockout effective period, select 30 minutes or more.

Title: Minimum password length
Remediation: 1. From Setup, enter Password Policies in the Quick Find box, then select Password Policies.2. Under Minimum password length, select 8 or more.

Title: Obscure secret answer for password resets
Remediation: 1. From Setup, enter Password Policies in the Quick Find box, then select Password Policies.2. Select Obscure secret answer for password resets.

Title: Password question requirement
